openingQuery: What linen do you have for 10 days in Goa?

chat.content: What linen do you have for 10 days in Goa?

Goa in linen — you'll want pieces that breathe, pack flat, and forgive a ten-day wear. Three I'd pull: the Pellier Linen Shirt in ecru ($148), the Linen Drawstring Trousers in oat ($118), and the Italian Linen Camp Shirt in indigo ($228). All three crumple honestly rather than crease — that's the tell of real linen, not a blend.

toolCalls.description: Semantic search for linen travel pieces
toolCalls.sql: SELECT name, brand, price, 1 - (embedding <=> $1) AS similarity FROM product_catalog WHERE category ILIKE '%linen%' AND quantity > 0 ORDER BY embedding <=> $1 LIMIT 10;
toolCalls.resultSummary: Top 3 linen travel pieces returned by cosine distance

confidence.reasoning: Strong semantic match on 'linen' + 'travel'; Marco's LTM preference profile (natural fibers, warm tones) weights the Hadley / Pellier linen shirt and drawstring trousers highest.

memoryPills.content: Planning 10 days in Goa — linen, travels well
Prefers natural fibers; warm neutrals; pieces that soften

chat.content: What would go with the Hadley shirt?

The Hadley pairs beautifully with the Drawstring Trousers in oat — same washed-linen family, same tonal register, they read as a considered set rather than an accident. For layering when the evening cools, the Washed Cotton Overshirt in ecru picks up the same muted palette without adding bulk. And the Leather Weekender ties the whole packing list together — the kind of bag that earns its patina on the trip.

toolCalls.description: Product-to-product pgvector similarity from Hadley / Pellier Linen Shirt
toolCalls.sql: SELECT p2.name, p2.price, 1 - (p1.embedding <=> p2.embedding) AS similarity FROM product_catalog p1, product_catalog p2 WHERE p1.product_id = $1 AND p2.product_id != $1 AND p2.quantity > 0 ORDER BY p1.embedding <=> p2.embedding LIMIT 5;
toolCalls.resultSummary: 3 companion pieces returned by product-to-product similarity

confidence.reasoning: the-packing-list skill loaded; style_match returned companions from Marco's travel-linen cluster with strong cosine similarity.

memoryPills.content: Interested in pairings for the Hadley shirt

chat.content: What's the price range for linen shirts?

Linen shirts in the catalog run from $88 to $285. Median is $148. The Pellier sits right at the median; the Italian Linen Camp Shirt ($228) is in the upper third. Under $100 there are two — both cotton-linen blends rather than pure linen.

toolCalls.description: Price distribution for linen shirts category
toolCalls.sql: SELECT MIN(price), MAX(price), AVG(price), PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY price) AS median FROM product_catalog WHERE category ILIKE '%linen%' AND name ILIKE '%shirt%';

confidence.reasoning: Deterministic aggregate. Claude Haiku 4.5 at 0.1 — no variance, sub-200ms.

chat.content: Is the Hadley shirt at the Brooklyn warehouse?

I can help with style and recommendations, but I don't have real-time stock visibility for individual warehouses yet. I can tell you the Hadley shirt (Pellier Linen Shirt in ecru) is in the catalog and marked in-stock system-wide — but which warehouse holds it, and how many are on the floor, sits outside what I can answer right now.

plan.flowSummary: Dispatcher → stock intent matched → Stock Keeper stubbed → voice-matched non-answer

confidence.reasoning: Stock intent matched by the Dispatcher (keywords: warehouse, available, at the). Stock Keeper is in stub state — no agent invoked. Fall-through returned the voice-matched non-answer.

memoryPills.content: Asked about warehouse stock — not answered

meta.note: Pattern III fall-through — Stock Keeper is the workshop build

chat.content: I'll come back when I'm ready to commit.

telemetry.note: Click 'Open the Stock Keeper build' to jump to the challenge.

sections.title: The arc
paragraphs: Marco arrived with a clear plan — ten days in Goa, linen — and walked through the catalog in four turns. Three landed cleanly. The fourth didn't.

The Dispatcher routed each turn to exactly one specialist: Style Advisor on Claude Opus 4.6 for the opening ask, Curator on Claude Opus 4.6 with the-packing-list skill loaded for pairings, Value Analyst on Claude Haiku 4.5 for the price range. All three specialists running on their declared models; no normalization.

sections.title: The gap
paragraphs: Turn 4 — the warehouse-stock question — sits outside the system's current reach. The Dispatcher matched the intent, routed to Stock Keeper, and Stock Keeper answered with a voice-matched non-answer because the agent ships in stub state.
This is the workshop build. Participants wire floor_check (and in the full Workshop format, restock_shelf and running_low). Once wired, the same Turn 4 pill returns a real warehouse + count + ETA.

sections.title: Why this matters
paragraphs: The gap isn't broken code. It's a design choice made visible: you only ship the agents you can answer for. Until Stock Keeper has real warehouse data behind it, the honest answer is 'I can't help with that yet.'
